summary: The Pester suite thoroughly covers the three core module functions — manifest parsing (including devDependencies merge, empty manifest, missing file, invalid JSON), mock license lookup (happy path, absent key, case-insensitivity), license status classification (approved/denied/unknown, null/empty input, deny-wins conflict), and the integration report (counts, statuses, compliant flag). Edge cases are meaningfully represented: deny-overrides-allow, unknown deps not in DB, all-approved scenario, file-not-found and parse-error propagation. BeforeAll/AfterAll are used correctly; fixtures are shared sensibly via $script: scope; temp files are created and removed per-test. The main gaps are: (1) the CLI wrapper Invoke-LicenseCheck.ps1 — its exit codes (0/1/2), --OutPath behaviour, and JSON stdout format — has zero unit coverage; (2) scoped package names (@scope/pkg) are never exercised; (3) manifests with only devDependencies (no 'dependencies' key at all) and empty allow/deny lists are untested; (4) the integration Describe calls New-ComplianceReport redundantly for each assertion rather than capturing one result in BeforeAll, causing minor fragility if setup cost grows. Overall a strong, readable suite that would catch most regressions in the module layer.
